prompt: The socket connection hangs indefinitely. What is the bug?
choices: Socket.getInputStream() does not work for HTTP connections
No timeout is set; if the server keeps the connection open (HTTP keep-alive), read() blocks forever waiting for more data
in.read() returns bytes, not characters, so char casting is incorrect
Socket must be closed before reading from it
explanation: Without a socket timeout, read() blocks indefinitely waiting for the server to close the connection or send more data. HTTP/1.1 servers often keep connections alive. Fix: s.setSoTimeout(5000) sets a 5-second read timeout, causing a SocketTimeoutException if no data arrives in time.
